title: Dez Multipurpose Project (Dez Dam) -- Key Engineering, Investment and Schedule Parameters (World Bank Appraisal, 1960)
source_org: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (World Bank)
source_url: https://openknowledge.worldbank.org (World Bank Archives, Iran country documents)
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION from narrative text (Summary and Conclusions + Introduction sections, PDF pages 1-4 of data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1960_dez_multipurpose_project_appraisal.pdf). Text was cleanly extractable via `pdftotext -layout` for these narrative pages (no visual/PNG verification needed -- narrative prose renders reliably in this document, unlike the tabular Annex pages which required PNG rendering; see companion dataset wb1960dez-annex1-dam-power-cost-estimate for that methodology). Every value quoted or closely paraphrased from the source text; illegible or cut-off figures (e.g. exact reservoir useful-life figure) are flagged as such in the notes column rather than estimated.
time_coverage: 1957 (project conception) - 1960 (appraisal date) with forward schedule to 1974 (full-scheme completion target) and 1984 (power facilities planning horizon)
frequency: single cross-section / project profile (not a time series)
units: mixed -- see unit column per row (meters, million cubic meters, MW, hectares, million USD, million Rials)
license_terms: World Bank Archives — public disclosure authorized document
notes: Source: 'IRAN -- Appraisal of the Dez Multipurpose Project' (World Bank, October 1960 report). The Dez Dam (later officially the Mohammad Reza Shah Pahlavi Dam) was Iran's flagship Pahlavi-era multipurpose dam -- first step of a planned long-range Khuzistan development program, financed with World Bank involvement, designed as a thin concrete arch dam 190m high with an underground powerhouse, built to serve irrigation (110,000 ha full-scheme target vs. 20,000 ha initial pilot), hydroelectric power (520 MW ultimate, 8 x 65MW units), and flood control for the Ahwaz-to-Persian-Gulf zone. Development and Resources Corporation (DRC) of New York was the principal consultant (retained from 1957); Electroconsult (dam design/construction supervision) and Nederlandsche Heidemaatschappij of Arnhem (irrigation design) were sub-consultants. Companion dataset: wb1960dez-annex1-dam-power-cost-estimate (detailed cost breakdown). Part of this project's systematic Pahlavi-era World Bank PDF mining effort (Round 38, mining/dams/industrial hunt). Cross-reference: the dam's actual completion/operational history (it began operating 1963) and later renaming are not covered by this 1960 pre-construction appraisal document -- a future pass could seek a project completion report analogous to the Ghazwin (Qazvin) 1978 completion report already in this archive, if one exists for Dez.
